title: Sword of Ajunta Pall

{{Quote|My sword... I filled it with my pride. And it is... buried with me, now. A corpse as I am a corpse. I am dead, as my faith... is dead. And I shall remain here... surrounded by blackness in death as in life...|[[Ajunta Pall]], to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
The '''sword of Ajunta Pall''' was used by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Ajunta Pall]] during the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], and was laid to rest with him in his [[Tomb of Ajunta Pall|tomb]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].

Ajunta Pall built a fierce reputation during and after his time. One of the ways he did this was through his [[double-bladed sword]]. Some people believed that it was his sword that gave Ajunta his power, but Pall himself thought that it was the source of his corruption.

==Description==
{{Quote|'I am that which grips the heart in fright, hearkens night and silences the light.' It was written of my sword, long…long ago.|Ajunta Pall commenting on his sword to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
The blade of this sword was described as being ebony black in color and notched along its length to give the appearance of being cruel and jagged. It was also said to be very cool to the touch and that upon closer inspection a small insignia of a dark circle could be seen on the hilt. The sentence "I am that which grips the heart in fright, hearkens night and silences the light" was written on the sword.<ref name="KotOR" />

==History==
{{Quote|According to legend, the blade proved more fearsome than the Master, leading to his demise. Ajunta's dark specter lived on through the centuries, until Revan entered the Sith Lord's tomb in search of the blade. Revan calmed the angry ghost of Ajunta Pall and showed him the path back to the light.|[[Kreia]]|<ref name="TSL">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
During the [[Jedi Civil War]], the redeemed Dark Lord of the Sith [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] entered Pall's tomb to get this sword in hopes he could gain [[prestige]] in the eyes of the [[Sith Master/Legends|master]] of the nearby [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]], [[Uthar Wynn]] (though Revan's main mission through these actions was to find the [[Star Map]]).<ref name="KotOR" />

Revan found the double-bladed sword, along with two ordinary swords, in Ajunta's sarcophagus. After a conversation with the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of the long dead Sith Lord, he correctly decided which sword was the correct one. After doing this, Revan showed Pall the way back to the light side, finally calming his tortured soul. Before leaving, however, Pall warned Revan about the corruption within the sword. He told Revan that if he was wise, he would not keep the sword, in order to avoid a similar fate.<ref name="KotOR" />

As Revan and his companions left the tomb, they were accosted by another Sith hopeful, [[Shaardan]]. He had planned to let someone else reach the sword after going through the deadly traps, and then purloin the sword for himself. Revan refused to hand it over, and Shaardan attacked him; the brief encounter ended with Shaardan's demise. Revan returned the blade to Uthar Wynn, and upon recounting the events of the brief skirmish with his rival, he gained quite a bit of prestige in the eyes of the Sith Master.<ref name="KotOR" /><ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

When [[Meetra Surik]] arrived on Korriban five years later, [[Kreia]] told Revan's tale to Surik. She stated it was unknown what Revan had done with the ancient artifact.<ref name="TSL" />

===Legacy===
Nearly four thousand years later, at the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Staff Sergeant/Legends|Staff Sergeant]] [[Rothax]] rewarded an Imperial soldier for several victorious battles against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] with a [[Sith sword]] that was rumored to be a close replica of Ajunta Pall's sword.<ref name="SWG:GU10">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other|Game Update 10}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Although canonically Revan killed Shaardan and gave the sword to Master Wynn, the player has several options with what to do with the sword and its fakes: the player can give one of the fake swords to Shaardan, who gleefully runs away without checking its authenticity, and is reprimanded at the Academy by Wynn for this, and [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|Force choked]] to death; the player can give the actual sword to Shaardan and lose out of prestige; or the player does not have to turn the sword over to Wynn (as it is possible to gain enough prestige without giving up any items they find), and they or one of their crew members can use it for the rest of the game.<ref name="KotOR" />

In spite of the Sword's prominence in his first appearance in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'',<ref name="KotOR" /> artwork portraying Ajunta Pall in sources published since ''Knights of the Old Republic'' shows him with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]],<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> and a single-bladed one at that.<ref name="BOS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>
